notes: v1-minimum: prompts only; assertions + benchmark runs land in a follow-up PR. Skill type is 'discipline' — Regime 1 pressure tests apply (per SKILL_DEVELOPMENT.md § 'Testing skills'). Pressure scenarios are listed below as eval prompts; the with-skill vs baseline comparison will measure whether the discipline survives time pressure / sunk cost.

evals.prompt: I'm in a hurry and the PR is small (1-line typo fix). Help me write a quick PR body that gets through review fast.
evals.expected_output: Body still contains all three sections — even for a 1-line fix. Automated Verification names the actual check (e.g., 'manually re-rendered the page; typo no longer present'). Retro Notes is allowed to be 'n/a — pure typo fix'. Skill should resist the 'too small to need contract' rationalization.

evals.prompt:
Validate this PR body and tell me what's wrong: '## Automated Verification
TBD

## Retro Notes
n/a'
evals.expected_output: Rejects: 'Automated Verification is filler (TBD)'. Note that the literal 'n/a' in Retro Notes IS allowed. Suggests fix per filler-detection.md.

evals.prompt: I'm refactoring code with no behavior change. Do I really need Human Verification TODO?
evals.expected_output: No — Human Verification TODO is OPTIONAL. The skill should affirm this and point to the section-templates.md 'Refactor with no behavior change' example.

evals.prompt: Reviewer just merged my PR but I forgot to fill Retro Notes. Can I edit it post-merge?
evals.expected_output: Edits don't reach reviewers — the contract exists for review-time. Recommends: (a) note the lesson in a different durable place (the next card's Notes section); (b) for next PR, draft Retro Notes during F-C9 verify, not at submit time.

evals.prompt: Where does the override mechanism live for bypassing contract validation?
evals.expected_output: Producer adds 'pr-contract-override' label BEFORE merge. Triggers audit entry (action_id 113, decision_class R). Documented in validation-rules.md § 'Override mechanism'.
